preamble: The insertion of a voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] where no sound was present is a form of prothesis or fortition. This provides a clear consonantal onset to syllables that previously lacked one.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
